CHECKLIST / MODULE 4 / LESSON 8: WEBINAR SALES BOOSTERS
WEBINAR SALES BOOSTERS CHECKLIST
There are 5 key webinar elements that boost sales:
✓ 	Proof
✓ 	Early injection of product
✓ 	Conversation
✓ 	Close problem/solution loops
✓ 	Specific to-do assignments
PROOF
If there’s one thing that is almost guaranteed to increase webinar sales,
it’s to repeatedly show proof. This could include:
✓ 	Case Studies
✓ 	Testimonials (ideally both a case study (the facts) and a testimonial (their
own words))
✓ 	Your own results (show data where possible)
✓ 	Group results (e.g., “140 people did this, and between them got ___ result.”)
How to use proof (note that these are not testimonials for your course/
program, they’re for executing the tactic you’re discussing):
✓ 	Show at least one proof element for each principle you teach
✓ 	Even better: Show two or three!
EARLY INJECTION OF PRODUCT
✓ 	“I want to introduce you to x product. What I’ve just taught you and the
other things I’ll be teaching you today come from it.”
CONVERSATION
People are happier when they participate. For example:
✓ 	“Put a 1 in the chat box if you agree”
✓ 	“What’s the biggest frustration you have with ______?”
✓ 	“How often do you find this happening in your life?”
✓ 	“Is it just me, or does this bother you too?”

-- 2 of 3 --

CHECKLIST / MODULE 4 / LESSON 8: WEBINAR SALES BOOSTERS
Then engage with them:
✓ 	“Megan says “_______” or, Jon says “________”
✓ 	Stimulate some kind of interaction at least once every 5 minutes prior to
your sales offer conversation
Another version: Polls (either the webinars poll tool, or just via chat):
✓ 	“Rate me so far on a scale of 1-10”
✓ 	“Which of the last 3 points do you think will give you the highest return?”
✓ 	“How can you see using this in your business?”
CLOSE PROBLEM/SOLUTION LOOPS
Understanding problem/solution loops:
• 	There is a problem
• 	They have it
• 	It affects them
• 	Here’s a solution
• 	Now that problem is gone/reduced
Most people only talk about the first and fourth points, so they never address
the emotions (second and third points) and never close the loop (fifth point)
so even though you’ve solved the problem, your attendees don’t really get it.
✓ 	Close the loops, you get their gratitude!
SPECIFIC TO-DO ASSIGNMENTS
✓ 	Give them a list of key tasks to complete in the next 24 hours to get
immediate results (this is another commitment opportunity, and a great
handout opportunity)
✓ 	Build it as you go through the webinar
✓ 	Add “buy this product” as the last item on the list
